content.rendered:
Some systems get confused when your server lives in one country and your team works in another. If your server’s in the USA but your office is in Asia, you might see attendance logs popping up at 3 AM — not ideal.

Attendo fixes this by separating your business logic from the server’s clock. Your attendance, schedules, and reports always follow the branch’s own timezone, exactly the way they should.

Key Capabilities

Branch-Specific Timezones: Configure a unique timezone for every branch. The New York office runs on EST, while the Dubai office runs on GST. The system automatically adjusts shifts and attendance rules to match the local reality.

Accurate Attendance Logs: When an employee clocks in at 9:00 AM in their local time, the report reflects 9:00 AM—even if the server thinks it is midnight. No more manual calculations or mental math for HR.

Smart Shift Scheduling: Assign duties across borders without conflict. A “Morning Shift” applies to the local morning of that specific employee, ensuring automation triggers (like “Late” or “Absent” flags) fire at the correct local moment.

Unified Reporting: Admins can view reports that are normalized. You see the exact local time the action occurred, preserving the context of the employee’s workday.

How It Works

  1. Setup: Admin assigns a Timezone to a specific Branch (e.g., “Asia/Dhaka”).
  2. Operation: An employee in that branch clocks in. The system captures the timestamp.
  3. Processing: Attendo converts and stores the data relative to the branch setting.
  4. Result: Reports show the correct local time for the employee, ensuring payroll calculation (overtime/late hours) is 100% accurate.
